---
title: "Andres Romero-Bravo"
subtitle: "Plant evolutionary ecologist | Lund University"
image: images/profile_photo.jpg
about:
  template: jolla
  links:
    - icon: github
      href: https://github.com/andresarb
    - icon: envelope
      href: mailto:andres.romero_bravo@biol.lu.se
---

Hola! I am Andrés, a plant evolutionary biologist focused on floral evolution, plant–pollinator interactions, and the evolvability of flowering plants. I am interested in studying the ecological and evolutionary mechanisms that determine the responses of flowering plants to environmental change.

In my research, I combine quantitative genetics, phenotypic plasticity, and field ecology to understand how reproductive and vegetative traits respond to changes in abiotic and biotic environments.

I hold a PhD from the University of Sussex, where I investigated floral plasticity, evolvability, and local adaptation under novel pollination environments. My work combines common-garden, transplant, and resurrection experiments with genomic and multivariate statistical approaches.

Beyond research, I am a co-founder and Secretary of [Jóvenes por la Botánica Española (JxBE)](https://jxbe.weebly.com/), a society for young botanists in Spain, where I am involved in the organisation of activities and events to promote botany among early-career researchers.

I am also committed to environmental education and science communication, drawing on my background in primary education to bridge academia and the wider public. Since 2018, I run [La naturaleza al detalle](https://www.lanaturalezaaldetalle.com), a nature photography blog where I combine images and educational text to share my passion for the natural world with a broad audience.
